{{Quote|I'm from the planet Aaeton, only half-day's journey from here. Young people from my planet often go on survival camping trips on Ragoon-6 when we reach fourteen years of age. We have a special allowance from the Senate because we gave the elders of Ragoon refuge when they handed the planet over to the Senate.|Floria|<ref name="JQ2">''[[Jedi Quest: The Trail of the Jedi]]''</ref>}}
'''Aaeton''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of the [[Aaeton system/Legends|Aaeton system]], in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] region. During the [[Cold War (Galactic Republic–Sith Empire)|Cold War]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|reconstituted Sith Empire]], an [[Unidentified Imperial bioweapon|Imperial bioweapon]] activated on the planet. During the last [[Standard day/Legends|days]] of the Republic, the government of Aaeton had authorization to allow its citizens to visit the generally off-limits neighboring world of [[Ragoon VI]].

==Description==
Aaeton was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="JQ2" /> that was located in the [[Aaeton system/Legends|Aaeton system]], situated in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core]] region of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="Atlas Online Companion" />

==History==
At some point before the stand-off conflict named the [[Cold War (Galactic Republic–Sith Empire)|Cold War]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|reconstituted Sith Empire]], the Republic established settlements on Aaeton. During that conflict, a dormant [[Unidentified Imperial bioweapon|Imperial bioweapon]] [[Bioweapon attack on Aaeton|activated]] on the planet, causing the rapid spread of [[Poison/Legends|toxic]] molds through the settlements.<ref name="SWTOR" />

{{Gamemechanics}}
An individual affiliated with the Republic visited Aaeton during that [[Time/Legends|time]] to assist with the situation.<ref name="SWTOR" />
{{Endgame}}

In the final [[Standard day/Legends|days]] of the Republic, the ruling government of Aaeton had exclusive permission to allow its citizens to visit the normally off-limits [[Ragoon VI]], a world half-a-day's [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] journey away.<ref name="TCSWE">{{CSWECite|I|4|Aaeton}}</ref> In [[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]],<ref name="ERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Floria]], when lying to the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] about her [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] being Aaeton, claimed that the reason the government had been given the authorization from the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]], the legislative branch of the Republic, was because of Aaeton's refuge of [[Ragoon/Legends|Ragoon VI's natives]] after the latter handed their homeworld to the Senate. According to Floria, the young people from Aaeton—after reaching an age of fourteen years—would often travel to Ragoon VI on survival camping journeys with that permission, being among the few off-worlders allowed on the planet.<ref name="JQ2" />

==Behind the scenes==
The planet Aaeton was first mentioned in the [[2003]] young readers novel ''[[Jedi Quest: The Trail of the Jedi]]'', part of the [[Star Wars: Jedi Quest|''Jedi Quest'']] series. In the novel, the planet is described by Floria, who first claims that Aaeton is her homeworld but eventually reveals that she was lying and that her homeworld is, in fact, [[Thracior]], casting doubt on the rest of her information about the planet.<ref name="JQ2" />

Aaeton was later mentioned in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', published in [[2008]].<ref name="TCSWE" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Aaeton [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet, in grid square K-9.<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> Aaeton has been most recently mentioned in ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'', a [[Star Wars video games|massively multiplayer online roleplaying game]] developed by [[BioWare]] and released in [[2011]], in which it was used as a possible location for Republic players to assign their companions on individual quests.<ref name="SWTOR" />
